events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess roofing nail placement high/exposed. Residential roofing, rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Nail placement high/exposed likely means shingles nailed too high (above nail line) or exposed nails, which violates manufacturer specs and IRC R905.2.5? Asphalt shingles require nails located per manufacturer, typically within marked zones. Exposed nails can cause leaks, wind uplift. So fail. Risk high? Could be high due to water intrusion and wind. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80. Confidence high. Findings: nails placed above manufacturer-specified nail line/exposed; not sealed. Actions: remove/replace affected shingles, renail per manufacturer within nail zone, seal/cover exposed fasteners. Rationale. References: IRC R905.2.5, R905.2.6, manufacturer installation instructions. Need output exactly JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

events.label: response

  "findings": [
    "Roofing fasteners are placed high and/or left exposed, outside the manufacturer’s designated nail zone.",
    "Improper nail placement reduces wind-uplift resistance and creates potential water-intrusion points.",
    "Installation does not conform to IRC R905 or the shingle manufacturer’s written instructions."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and replace affected shingles.",
    "Renail fasteners within the manufacturer-specified nail zone at the proper depth and spacing.",
    "Cover or seal any required exposed fasteners with approved sealant or cap as specified by the manufacturer.",
    "Re-inspect before closing out the roof."
  ],
  "rationale": "Asphalt shingles must be installed according to the manufacturer’s instructions and IRC R905, which require nails in the designated nail line/zone and properly concealed. High or exposed nail placement compromises the roof’s weather resistance and wind performance, so the work is non-compliant and defective.",
  "references": [
    "IRC R905.2.5 - Fasteners",
    "IRC R905.2.6 - Attachment",
    "Manufacturer shingle installation instructions"
  ]
}
